description: §61.7 — an `<endpoint>` declaration with no `path=` fires E-ENDPOINT-PATH-MISSING. The `path=` is the author-stable contract URL a foreign client connects to; without it the typed-inbound handler has no mount address.
rationale: §61.7: an `<endpoint>` SHALL declare a `path=` (the contract URL, the §12.3/§37.3 foreign-facing-endpoint rule reused). A missing `path=` is E-ENDPOINT-PATH-MISSING (parse-time, W2).
